rationale: The citing paper discusses Naik and Raman (2003) in the IMC literature review as one of the main prior studies on multimedia synergy and dynamic budgeting. It then explicitly uses that paper to define a limitation - market-level analysis without sequential lead-lag realities - and positions its own contribution as addressing that gap. This is more than a passing mention because the cited work helps structure the problem framing and the claimed novelty of the three-stage model.
relation_description: The 2006 paper builds on Naik and Raman (2003) by extending IMC synergy modeling from aggregate dynamic budgeting to a sequential marketing-to-sales process model with lead generation, follow-up delay, appointment conversion, and sales closure. It preserves the synergy perspective but adds downstream timing and conversion dynamics at the marketing-sales interface.
